2604.12202.title: Latent patterns of urban mixing in mobility analysis across five global cities
2604.12202.paper_summary: This study leverages large-scale travel surveys for over 200,000 residents across Boston, Chicago, Hong Kong, London, and Sao Paulo. With rich individual-level data, we make systematic comparisons and reveal patterns in social mixing, which cannot be identified by analyzing high-resolution mobility data alone. Using the same set of data, inferring socioeconomic status from residential neighborhoods yield social mixing levels 16% lower than using self-reported survey data. Besides, individuals over the age of 66 experience greater social mixing than those in late working life (aged 55 to 65), lending data-driven support to the "second youth" hypothesis. Teenagers and women with caregiving responsibilities exhibit lower social mixing levels. Across the five cities, proximity to major transit stations reduces the influence of individual socioeconomic status on social mixing. Finally, we construct detailed spatio-temporal place networks for each city using a graph neural network. Inputs of home-space, activity-space and demographic attributes are embedded and fed into a supervised autoencoder to predict individual exposure vectors. Results show that the structure of individual activity space, i.e., where people travel to, explains most of the variations in place exposure, suggesting that mobility shapes experienced social mixing more than sociodemographic characteristics, home environment, and transit proximity. The ablation tests further discover that, while different income groups may experience similar levels of social mixing, their activity spaces remain stratified by income, resulting in structurally different social mixing experiences.
2604.12202.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究利用了超過200,000名居民的廣泛旅行調查，涵蓋波士頓、芝加哥、香港、倫敦和聖保羅。<lb/>憑藉豐富的個人層級數據，我們進行系統比較並揭示社會混合的模式，這些模式無法僅通過分析高解析度的流動性數據來識別。<lb/>使用相同的數據集，從居住社區推斷社會經濟地位所產生的社會混合水平比使用自我報告的調查數據低16%。<lb/>此外，66歲以上的個體經歷的社會混合程度高於接近退休年齡（55至65歲）的個體，為“第二青春”假說提供了數據驅動的支持。<lb/>青少年和有照顧責任的女性表現出較低的社會混合水平。<lb/>在這五個城市中，靠近主要交通站點減少了個人社會經濟地位對社會混合的影響。<lb/>最後，我們使用圖神經網絡為每個城市構建詳細的時空地點網絡。<lb/>家庭空間、活動空間和人口特徵的輸入被嵌入並輸入到一個監督式自編碼器中，以預測個體暴露向量。<lb/>結果顯示，個體活動空間的結構，即人們旅行的地方，解釋了地點暴露的大部分變異，這表明流動性對經歷的社會混合的影響超過了社會人口特徵、家庭環境和交通接近性。<lb/>消融測試進一步發現，儘管不同收入群體可能經歷相似的社會混合水平，但他們的活動空間仍然按收入分層，導致結構上不同的社會混合經驗。
2604.12202.author: Z. Fan et.al.
2604.12202.authors: Z. Fan, B. P. Y. Loo, F. Duarte, C. Ratti, E. Moro
